Page.Line: of votes cast in the state for and against such measure and question each
initiative and referendum and declaring such measures as are approved by a

Line.strikethrough: majority of those voted thereon to
Line.underline: approved an initiative that has received
at least sixty percent (60%) of the aggregate number of votes cast and a ref-
erendum that has received an affirmative majority of the aggregate number of

Page.Line: votes cast. An initiative or referendum so approved shall be in full force
and effect as the law of the state of Idaho from the effective date provided

Line.underline: in the initiative or referendum, or, if no effective date is provided, from

Page.Line: the date of said the governor's proclamation for any referendum measure.

Line.strikethrough: The effective date for an initiative measure shall be governed by the provi-

Page.Line: sions of subsection (2) of this section. If two (2) or more measures shall
be initiatives or referendums are approved at said election which that are
known to confl ict with each other or to contain conflicting provisions, he
the governor shall also proclaim which is paramount in accordance with the
provisions of sections 34-1801 through 34-1822, Idaho Code.

Line.strikethrough: (2)(a) A statewide initiative may contain an effective date, if passed,
that shall be no earlier than July 1 of the year following the vote on the
ballot initiative. If no effective date is specified in the petition,
the effective date of a statewide initiative that has been approved by
the electorate shall be July 1 of the following year.

Page.Line: (b) (2) A city or county initiative may contain an effective date, if
passed, that may be earlier than July 1 of the year following the vote on the
ballot city or county initiative, but no earlier than the mayor's proclama-
tion as provided in section 34-1801B, Idaho Code, or the proclamation by the
board of county commissioners, as provided in section 34-1801C, Idaho Code.
If no effective date is specified in the petition, the effective date of a
city or county initiative that has been approved by the electorate shall be
July 1 of the following year.
SECTION 3. An emergency existing therefor, which emergency is hereby
declared to exist, this act shall be in full force and effect on and after its
passage and approval.
